project_name: Batched Reader QETS Mixed-Domain Scaling Validation

decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as supported; only open a new project for a materially distinct generative-reader or production-serving validation.
decision.stop_reason: Batched mixed-domain HF-reader validation preserved QETS quality while saving 46.3% prompt tokens overall and improving QETS reader throughput from 37.0 to 59.0 packs/s at batch size 16.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as supported; only open a new project for a materially distinct generative-reader or production-serving validation.
run_notes_tail: - Updated `README.md` with the batched mixed-domain command and headline result.
Observed 320-example mixed-domain result at batch size 16 (`results/mixed_domain_batched_320_budget065_b16_v2.json`):
| slice | n | token reduction | answer F1 delta pts | EM delta pts | support recall delta pts | success |
- Batch size 1 (`results/mixed_domain_batched_320_budget065_b1.json`): vanilla reader 23.6 packs/s, QETS reader 37.0 packs/s; total wall 28.37s, CPU 99%, max RSS 1.22GB.
- Batch size 16 (`results/mixed_domain_batched_320_budget065_b16_v2.json`): vanilla reader 27.1 packs/s, QETS reader 59.0 packs/s; total wall 23.45s, CPU 99%, max RSS 1.34GB.
- Batch size 16 monitoring (`results/gpu_monitor_mixed_batched_320_budget065_b16_v2.csv`): 45 samples, mean GPU utilization 51.5%, max 79%, mean power 58.4W.
- UMA-aware memory at validation end: `/proc/meminfo` showed MemAvailable ~121.8GB and SwapFree 0; process max RSS stayed around 1.34GB.

- The branch kill condition is not met. Batching preserved the parent quality signal on the calibrated mixed-domain sample, and the same QETS packs retained >=45% token savings with non-negative F1 in each domain and overall.

- Batching improves the scaling profile but the Hugging Face pipeline still reports a v5 deprecation warning for list-style SQuAD inputs, so future engineering should migrate the reader call API before relying on this harness long-term.

- This successor branch has enough durable evidence to finalize positive. A further branch is not needed unless the next mechanism changes to a generative reader/judge or a larger production-serving stack.
